title: quercus breviloba subsp. pseudocrispata
intro: Quercus breviloba subsp. pseudocrispata, also known as the Korean white oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and small acorns. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus breviloba subsp. pseudocrispata include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Quercus breviloba subsp. pseudocrispata can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
